mechanism_narrative: Higher levels of the federal funds rate increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings and offer a more competitive risk-free return, leading to lower equity valuations. This effect is persistent and acts as a headwind for forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:As of 2024-01-15, FEDFUNDS is at a restrictive level of 5.33%
failure_condition: The correlation between the level of FEDFUNDS and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate from its recent low is a strong leading indicator of a recession (Sahm's Rule). This signals a sharp deterioration in the labor market and aggregate demand, causing negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-month moving average of UNRATE rises > 0.5pp above its 12-month low
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20d returns in more than 50% of instances when the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously slowing retail sales growth and accelerating inflation create a stagflationary environment. This erodes corporate profit margins and reduces real consumer purchasing power, leading to negative equity market performance.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year % change in RSAFS < 2% AND Year-over-year % change in CPIAUCSL > 4%
evidence_refs: fact:1970s stagflationary period was poor for equities
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward Information Coefficient (IC) is not statistically significantly negative (p > 0.05) over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a deceleration in industrial production growth indicates that restrictive policy is successfully cooling economic activity. This slowdown in the goods-producing sector precedes broader earnings weakness, negatively impacting stock returns.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO growth has been slowing in late 2023 during a tightening cycle
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is rising, a negative 3-month change in INDPRO is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 60% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a robust and expanding economy. This strength translates into higher aggregate demand and corporate revenues, supporting positive equity returns over a short-term horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:December 2023 PAYEMS data showed continued job growth
failure_condition: The 12-month rolling correlation between the 1-month change in PAYEMS and 5-day forward S&P 500 returns becomes consistently negative (<-0.1).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of high inflation (CPIAUCSL) and a high federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates that the central bank is actively fighting inflation with restrictive policy. This environment of tight financial conditions is a strong headwind for equities, leading to negative returns.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year % change in CPIAUCSL > 3.5% AND FEDFUNDS > 4.0%
evidence_refs: fact:Current conditions as of Jan 2024 meet this trigger
failure_condition: When the trigger condition is met, the S&P 500 has a positive average 60-day forward return over a 3-year sample period.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts (HOUST) is a leading indicator of a slowdown in the interest-rate sensitive sectors of the economy. This signals weakening consumer confidence and investment, which precedes broader economic weakness and negative stock returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing sector weakness preceded the 2008 financial crisis
failure_condition: In periods following a trigger event, 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) is the broadest measure of economic expansion. This growth underpins corporate earnings expectations, driving positive equity returns over a medium-term horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Latest GDPC1 vintage shows continued economic expansion
failure_condition: The rank correlation between quarterly real GDP growth and subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns is not statistically positive (p > 0.10) over a full business cycle.
